(21:43:54) Gary Wong: Hi, this shop owner allows customers to use their own UPS shipping account. They want us to collect that customer's UPS account information at order time (probably in the cart), let user select "Use my UPS account" as shipping method (how to show price?), then have the shipping label use that customer's UPS account. Is this possible at all with PHMultiCarrier?

comments.body:
I see that I can set up an automation rule to set a third-party UPS billing account for a shipment. When that happens, does it simply use the same calculated shipment packages and rates that the customer saw during checkout to generate the label, but simply charged to the third-party billing account?


Thank you,

Gary Wong
www.spingroup.com
comments.html_body:
I see that I can set up an automation rule to set a third-party UPS billing account for a shipment. When that happens, does it simply use the same calculated shipment packages and rates that the customer saw during checkout to generate the label, but simply charged to the third-party billing account?

Thank you,

Gary Wong
www.spingroup.com
comments.plain_body:
I see that I can set up an automation rule to set a third-party UPS billing account for a shipment. When that happens, does it simply use the same calculated shipment packages and rates that the customer saw during checkout to generate the label, but simply charged to the third-party billing account? 
 
 

Thank you,



Gary Wong

www.spingroup.com

comments.body:
Hello Gary,

Thank you for your reply.

Yes, your understanding is correct.

Setting up an automation rule with the third-party billing option will ensure that the shipping charges are billed to the specified UPS account instead of your own UPS account. The calculated rates will continue to be displayed using your UPS account.

Please note that an automation rule configured with a third-party billing account will apply to all orders that match the rule conditions. We can create rules based on criteria such as shipping zone, order value, total weight, and more. However, it is not possible to apply a third-party billing account to a specific individual order.

If you are looking for any specific flow, please let us know we can review it once

Please let us know how it goes or if you need any assistance with the setup.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello Gary,

Thank you for your reply.

We can set rules based on conditions such as total weight, shipping zone, order value, etc. However, at present, it is not possible to create a rule based on a specific customer. If this functionality is introduced in the future, we will keep you posted.

Please feel free to reach out to us anytime if you need any assistance from our end.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

Please feel free to reach out to us anytime if you need any assistance from our end.

comments.body:
Would it be possible for your team to add a function to the Orders area to apply a third party UPS account billing code (if the order uses UPS carrier)?

Then when generating the label, you can simply add the third party billing account number and address (BillThirdParty) and info to the JSON request.

My client ships about 35% of their shipments using their customers’ UPS accounts, and they get new ones every day so they don’t want to have to set them up ahead of time.

Is this something your team might be willing to add? Otherwise, my client will have to find a different app/service (like ShipStation) than PHMC and we would have to start all over again with shipping and fulfillment setup!

A bonus would be that, when we enter the 3rd party UPS account # and address, that it would check to make sure it is valid via the UPS Account Validation API.

Thank you for your help,

Gary Wong
gary.wong@spingroup.com

comments.body:
🤖 Auto-generated draft — customer reply (probation mode)
Product: shopify-mcsl | Signal: form_field | Confidence: HIGH | Context: 10 message(s)
─────────────────────────────────────────────

Hello Gary,

Thank you for confirming the store — we have noted cameronpackaging.myshopify.com as the production account for this request.

To answer the question you raised earlier: the ability to enter a per-order third-party UPS billing account number directly from the Orders area is not a current feature of the app. As Manohar mentioned, the existing third-party billing support works through pre-configured automation rules, which requires the account number to be set up in advance — not a practical fit for your client's scenario where new customer UPS accounts arrive daily.

We have captured your full request, including the per-order billing account field, the BillThirdParty data flow you described, and the UPS Account Validation API suggestion. This will be shared with our product team for review. Please do send along the monthly order volume when your client has it — that context helps the team assess priority.

We will follow up once the product team has had a chance to review the feasibility. We appreciate you taking the time to outline the use case in detail.

Thanks & Regards,
Team PluginHive

⚡ This response was generated automatically by PluginHive AI Support. A team member will follow up if you need further assistance.

comments.body:
Hello Gary Wong,

Thank you for your time.

While I am reviewing this requirement internally with our team, we would like to understand a few additional details before we proceed further and check the available possibilities:
1. Approximately how many orders does your client process per month?
2. How will the customer's UPS account details be collected for third-party billing?
3. Will these shipments be domestic only, or will they also include international shipments?


Once we receive this information, we will discuss the requirement with our team and get back to you with an update.

I look forward to hearing from you.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello,

> 1. Approximately how many orders does your client process per month?

My client said they run about 100 orders per month.

> 2. How will the customer's UPS account details be collected for third-party billing?

Client is not on Shopify Plus, so I cannot do anything in the checkout. So my plan is to add questions/fields to the Cart page and save the data with the order as meta data. I will collect:
1. Use My UPS Account [boolean]
2. My UPS account # [text]
3. My UPS account postal code [numeric]
4. Add clear instructions on how to get these numbers from UPS.

I will write and deploy a Shopify Function which will add a 100% discount to shipping cost if customer "Use My UPS Account" is true.

I will add a post-order flow which will add a tag to the order labeled "Use Customer UPS Account".

I will add a View in PHMC Orders listing area to filter for these orders by this tag so they can easily be discerned from the other orders.

Ideally, I’d like to put the order on HOLD so they don’t accidentally get shipped without 3rd party account number. However, the PHMC Order import ignores the HOLD and sets all orders to Processing. Would be great if there is a HOLD status in PHMC that matches the actual order status.

When the shipment is selected for shipping, staff will assign 3rd party UPS account to the order (the feature you are hopefully adding). It would be important to be able to see the 3rd party UPS account # in Order Detail and Order Listing as well.

> 3. Will these shipments be domestic only, or will they also include international shipments?

Only US accounts are allowed so we set UPS Account country code to US.

Thank you!

Gary Wong
gary.wong@spingroup.com

comments.body:
Hello Gary,

Thank you for your reply and for sharing the details.

For UPS third-party billing, the following information is required:
- UPS account number
- Complete billing address, including street address, city, postal code, state, and country

Could you please confirm whether this information is being collected?

Also, would you be willing to subscribe to the **$29 Popular Plan** or a higher plan? Please find the plan details attached.

Regarding the **Hold** status, the app imports all unfulfilled Shopify orders by default. However, this can be managed by adding filters based on order tags and saving those filters. I will be happy to guide you through the setup, so no worries.

Looking forward to hearing back from you.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

 ![](https://pluginhive.zendesk.com/attachments/token/or6FXa0TOObzCNaaRv3HhuMDI/?name=image.png)

Hi Manohar,

> - Complete billing address, including street address, city, postal code, state, and country
> Could you please confirm whether this information is being collected?

Yes, we will collect those extra fields too for 3rd party billing. We have not built that part yet, but we will if we can get all this to work with PHMC.

> Also, would you be willing to subscribe to the **$29 Popular Plan** or a higher plan? Please find the plan details attached.

Is the idea to add a tag to orders that are on HOLD, and then add a filter for it in PHMC? And then remove the tag once the order is taken off of HOLD status?

Thank you,

Gary Wong

comments.body:
Hello Gary,

Thank you for your reply.

Regarding third-party billing, please allow me some time to discuss this with my team. I'll get back to you with an update.

Regarding the hold orders, your understanding is correct. We can filter orders based on tags and process all pending hold orders that require third-party billing first, and then process the remaining orders.

There is no need to remove the tag for third-party billing orders, as it can be useful for identifying all third-party billing orders later. You can maintain a dedicated tag for third-party billing orders, which will allow you to create filters for both pending and fulfilled orders whenever needed.

Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in the meantime.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Gary,
 
Thank you for your reply.
 
Regarding third-party billing, please allow me some time to discuss this with my team. I'll get back to you with an update.
 
Regarding the hold orders, your understanding is correct. We can filter orders based on tags and process all pending hold orders that require third-party billing first, and then process the remaining orders.
 
There is no need to remove the tag for third-party billing orders, as it can be useful for identifying all third-party billing orders later. You can maintain a dedicated tag for third-party billing orders, which will allow you to create filters for both pending and fulfilled orders whenever needed.
 
Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in the meantime.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
**Need assistance from L3 team**

**Case:** UPS Third-Party Billing – Feature request / feasibility check

**Customer Details:**

- **Plan:** $29 Popular Plan (MCSL)
- **Store URL:** kqhyjj-jj.myshopify.com
- **Account ID:** 97b3cc3d-1a4c-491e-b84d-e4e45ed86999


**Background:**
The customer wants to process approximately **30% of their orders using UPS Third-Party Billing**, where the shipping charges are billed to the customer's UPS account.

Currently, UPS Third-Party Billing is available through **Automation Rules**, which can be configured to apply to all orders or based on conditions such as zone, order value, etc.

However, the customer's workflow is different:

- They offer **Third-Party Billing** as a shipping option/cart option on their storefront.
- When shoppers select this option, they collect:
  - UPS Account Number
  - Complete billing address associated with the UPS account

- They offer **Free Shipping** for these orders since the shipping cost is billed to the customer's UPS account.
- This information is stored in the Shopify order.


**Customer Requirement:**
Instead of relying on automation rules, the customer would like an **order-level option** similar to the existing **FedEx Special Services**.

For example:
More Actions > **UPS Third-Party Billing >**Enter the UPS account number and billing details >Generate the shipping label for **that specific order only**

This would allow them to decide on a per-order basis whether to use third-party billing.

We already have a similar workflow for **FedEx** under **More Actions > FedEx Special Services**. Could we implement a similar option for **UPS**?

This feature would likely benefit other customers with similar third-party billing workflows as well.

Could the L3 team please confirm whether this is technically feasible and, if not currently supported, whether it could be considered as a feature enhancement?

comments.body:
Hello Gary Wong,

Thank you for your patience while I checked this case with our product team.

I'm pleased to inform you that we will be enhancing the app to support **third-party billing** in the **Bulk Actions** section on the Orders page. This will allow you to select orders, apply third-party billing, and generate labels in bulk.

This enhancement is expected to go live within **3 to 4 weeks**. Rest assured, we'll keep you updated on the progress.

In the meantime, you or your client can continue processing regular orders through the app and process third-party billing orders outside the app.

Please feel free to reach out to us anytime if you need any assistance.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive
